steps.title: Identify the passage's key claim
steps.content: Panama City residents use parks more (53.5%) than Fortaleza residents (35.7%), yet fewer Panama City residents live within a 10-minute walk of a park. The final sentence draws the explicit conclusion: "greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use." The word "alone" signals that proximity is not the full story.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.: the passage says nothing about researcher expectations. ✗
  • Choice B — It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.: no mention of survey errors anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.: the passage describes no outdated sources. ✗
  • Choice D — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to the residents.: directly restates the passage's conclusion that proximity "alone" cannot explain the gap. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
